item.title: As data centers seek to tap Texas’ energy, grid regulators are close to approving a new way of vetting requests
item.description: The Public Utility Commission of Texas on Thursday will weigh and vote on ERCOT’s proposal to streamline the power approval process for data centers.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Former employee’s ethics complaint alleges age discrimination in Rep. Troy Nehls’ office
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas students saw slight gains in math, stalled progress in reading, STAAR results show
item.dc:creator: Jaden Edison

item.title: TribCast: Deep in the heart of the Texas GOP convention
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Former Lt. Gov. nominee Mike Collier sues over Texas’ onerous rules for independent candidates
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: USDA has new partners in the fight against screwworm — drones, dogs and fungi
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Where is screwworm in Texas? Track cases here.
item.description: The New World screwworm poses a multibillion-dollar threat to the state’s cattle industry. We’re keeping track of where these cases are reported.
item.dc:creator: Berenice Garcia

item.title: First-of-its-kind GOP midterm convention coming to Dallas in September, Paxton says
item.description: The Republican National Committee has not yet announced the midterm convention or its location, but it has teased the event as an opportunity to put Trump on the ballot.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: UT fires public radio station leader who challenged university statements about festival security
item.description: KUT’s Debbie Hiott believes University of Texas officials terminated her because she publicly pushed back against administration claims that the station’s security plans for an inaugural festival were insufficient.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Priest

item.title: Elected Officials Directory
item.description: To help Texans know more about their representatives, we’ve gathered the bios, contact information and more for the occupants of the state’s highest offices.

item.title: Government Salaries Explorer
item.description: Review the compensation of hundreds of thousands of state and municipal employees in Texas.

item.title: Texas Public Schools Explorer
item.description: Review enrollment trends, demographics, teacher experience, pay and outcomes for all schools, and more.

item.title: Newly announced TribFest speakers grapple with the country’s hardest questions
item.dc:creator: Matt Ewalt

item.title: No fat to trim: Texas beef industry profits eaten away by new threat — the screwworm
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas Railroad Commission race pits oil field engineer against energy trader running on culture wars
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas Tech QB who placed bets will not play amid Big 12 lawsuit, legal threat from Paxton
item.dc:creator: Jessica Priest

item.title: A Muslim Texan sought to find his place in the party at the state GOP convention. He left in tears.

item.title: Texas stopped funding gambling addiction programs years ago. A surprising donor is helping fill the void.

item.title: At Texas GOP convention, friction overshadows talk of unity

item.title: Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick says James Talarico will “go to hell” for his view of the Bible

item.title: Midland police ID suspect in shooting that left one victim dead, 10 injured
